_schema.description: New Zealand trades-vendor coverage. Smaller market than US/UK/AU/CA but with strong NZ-built FSM ecosystem (Fergus, NextMinute, Tradify) and unique licensing landscape (EWRB, PGDB, LBP).

vendors.name: Fergus
vendors.tagline: NZ-built FSM (Auckland) — founded by an NZ plumber, 20K+ trades businesses NZ+AU
vendors.market_position: Fergus is the NZ trades-software success story — founded by a working NZ plumber, now 20K+ trades businesses across NZ + AU. Strongest job-costing and profitability features in the NZ/AU market. Native ACC, GST, PAYE handling, deep Xero/MYOB integration.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Founder is a working NZ plumber — product understanding of NZ trades workflow is genuine. ACC (Accident Compensation Corporation) handling native. NZ support timezone.
pros: Founder is working NZ plumber — domain expertise real
Strongest job-costing depth in NZ/AU market
cons: Best fit for businesses serious about job-profitability analysis (steeper learning curve)
Less specialized than Tradify for pure quoting/invoicing workflow
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$50-$150/user/month

vendors.name: NextMinute
vendors.tagline: NZ-built job management — 5,000 NZ/AU tradies, 4.9★ Google/Facebook, strong for residential builders
vendors.market_position: NextMinute is NZ-built job management software with 5,000+ NZ/AU tradies. Particularly strong fit for residential builders and carpenters needing project costing. 4.9★ Google + Facebook ratings — among the highest-rated NZ-origin FSM platforms by users.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Strongest residential-builder and carpentry focus among NZ FSM platforms. NZ support + ACC/GST/PAYE handling.
cons: Best fit for project-based trades vs pure service trades
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$60-$150/user/month

vendors.name: Tradify (NZ)
vendors.tagline: NZ-built FSM with global presence (NZ/AU/UK/US) — easiest onboarding of the NZ-origin platforms
vendors.market_position: Tradify is NZ-built and now operates globally (NZ/AU/UK/US). For NZ contractors, it represents the NZ-origin FSM that has scaled internationally. Easiest onboarding of the NZ-origin platforms. PartnerStack affiliate ($160/signup).
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ home-market product with global expansion proves the platform. NZ support + ACC/GST/PAYE compliance.
pros: Easiest onboarding among NZ FSM platforms
cons: Less specialized than Fergus for job-costing depth
Pricing higher than ServiceM8 at entry tier
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$45-$120/user/month

vendors.name: Builderscrack
vendors.tagline: NZ's leading trades marketplace — pay-per-lead model, NZ-only consumer audience
vendors.market_position: Builderscrack is the leading NZ-only trades marketplace. Smaller scale than UK Checkatrade or AU Hipages reflects NZ's smaller market size, but the platform dominates NZ consumer search for trades. Pay-per-lead model.
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ-only — no cross-market presence. Smaller pool of registered tradies (~50K) than UK/AU equivalents.
pros: Leading NZ trades marketplace by consumer audience
Pay-per-lead model means no annual commitment
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$5-$30 per lead

vendors.name: NZI Insurance
vendors.tagline: 160-year IAG-owned NZ insurance brand — tradie packages standard
vendors.market_position: NZI is one of NZ's longest-established insurance brands — 160-year history, IAG-owned. Tradie packages cover Public Liability, Tools, Vehicle. Strong claims infrastructure backed by IAG's NZ market dominance.
vendors.nz_specific_note: IAG ownership provides institutional stability. Public Liability typically NZ$1-5M coverage standard.
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$30-$100/month typical for tradies PL + tools

vendors.name: Vero Insurance
vendors.tagline: Major NZ underwriter — all-in-one tradies package, broker-distributed
vendors.market_position: Vero is one of NZ's major insurance underwriters — known for all-in-one tradies packages distributed through broker channels (Crombie Lockwood, Gallagher NZ). Strong fit for NZ tradies wanting bundled coverage in a single policy.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Broker-distributed primarily — not direct quoting. Crombie Lockwood and Gallagher NZ are common Vero brokers for tradies.
pros: Major NZ underwriter with mature claims
vendors.typical_pricing: Broker-quoted, varies

vendors.name: AMI Business
vendors.tagline: NZ IAG retail brand — Tradies Bundle accessible direct online
vendors.market_position: AMI is IAG's NZ retail brand — same parent as NZI but more consumer-facing. Tradies Bundle is direct-online quoting accessible for solo and small NZ trades. Lower pricing than NZI broker-distributed for simple cases.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Direct-online accessibility makes AMI a quick-quote option vs broker-distributed alternatives.
cons: Less coverage depth than broker-distributed Vero
vendors.typical_pricing: Online-quoted, varies by trade

vendors.name: iPayroll
vendors.tagline: NZ-built payroll — full PAYE/IRD compliant, cloud-native
vendors.market_position: iPayroll is NZ-built cloud payroll — full PAYE compliance with IRD (Inland Revenue Department), KiwiSaver handling, ACC levy calculation native. Strong fit for NZ SMB trades businesses wanting NZ-specific compliance without Xero subscription dependency.
vendors.nz_specific_note: IRD-compliant PAYE submissions native. KiwiSaver employer contributions handled. ACC levy auto-calculated.
pros: Cloud-native vs legacy NZ payroll options
cons: Pricing higher than free competitors (Thankyou Payroll)
vendors.typical_pricing: NZ$15-$50/month based on employee count

vendors.name: Smartly
vendors.tagline: NZ payroll specialist — SMB focus, IRD-compliant
vendors.market_position: Smartly is NZ payroll specialist with SMB focus. Full IRD compliance, KiwiSaver, leave management. Competes with iPayroll in the NZ SMB payroll segment.
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ SMB focus with full compliance. Comparable to iPayroll in core feature set.
cons: Smaller scale than iPayroll or Xero NZ
vendors.typical_pricing: Varies — comparable to iPayroll

vendors.name: EWRB (Electrical Workers Registration Board)
vendors.tagline: NZ electrical worker registration — ~38K registered, legally required
vendors.market_position: EWRB is NZ's mandatory electrical worker registration body. ~38,000 registered electrical workers in NZ. Registration is legally required for any electrical work — unlicensed work is criminal offense. EWRB issues different practitioner categories (Electrician, Electrical Service Technician, Electrical Inspector, etc.).
vendors.nz_specific_note: Registration is legally mandatory. Annual fees apply. Practising license required to actually work — different from registration.
pros: Legally mandatory — clear NZ regulatory pathway
cons: Annual fees + practising license costs stack
Practising license must be renewed regularly
No exceptions — every NZ electrical worker must be registered
vendors.typical_pricing: Annual registration + practising license fees vary

vendors.name: PGDB (Plumbers, Gasfitters and Drainlayers Board)
vendors.tagline: NZ plumbing/gas/drainage licensing — legally required, unlicensed work is illegal
vendors.market_position: PGDB is NZ's mandatory plumbing, gasfitting, and drainage licensing body. Legally required for any plumbing/gas/drainage work in NZ — unlicensed work is criminal offense. Issues licenses across three disciplines with separate certifications for each.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Equivalent of UK Gas Safe + plumbing licensing combined. Mandatory licensing across plumbing + gas + drainage disciplines.
cons: Annual fees per discipline add up
Strict enforcement — non-compliance has criminal consequences
vendors.typical_pricing: Annual licensing fees per discipline

vendors.name: NoCowboys
vendors.tagline: NZ trust-led trades directory built on customer-verified reviews — Builderscrack's main rival
vendors.market_position: NoCowboys is a leading New Zealand trades directory built around customer-verified reviews — builders, mechanics, painters, plumbers, and other tradespeople rated and reviewed by real customers. It is Builderscrack's most direct trust-led competitor, differentiating on review depth and a more curated tradie listing rather than pure job-bidding.
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ-only, with an annual listing/subscription model and verified reviews at its core — a reputation-driven approach versus Builderscrack's pay-per-lead bidding. Good for established Kiwi tradies who want a review-led profile rather than buying individual leads.
pros: Annual listing means predictable cost (vs per-lead)
cons: Smaller reach than Builderscrack's bidding marketplace
vendors.typical_pricing: Annual listing/subscription; varies by plan

vendors.name: PayHero
vendors.tagline: NZ payroll by FlexiTime — Holidays Act-compliant, automated payday filing, strong for variable-hours trades
vendors.market_position: PayHero (by FlexiTime) is New Zealand payroll software designed for accurate compliance — online timesheets, employee self-service, leave management, and automated payday filing to IRD. It is notably strong on MBIE Holidays Act compliance, with legislative updates applied automatically and a dedicated NZ support team, and it offers a trades-specific payroll product.
vendors.nz_specific_note: MBIE Holidays Act compliance is a notorious NZ pain point, especially for variable-hours and overtime crews. PayHero's timesheet-driven model and automatic legislative updates are built for exactly that, with payday filing to IRD, KiwiSaver, and ACC handled.
pros: NZ-built with strong MBIE Holidays Act compliance
Timesheets, self-service, and leave management built in
Dedicated trades-payroll product and NZ support
cons: Per-employee pricing adds up as the team grows
vendors.typical_pricing: From NZ$19 + $4/employee per month (base); Super $39 + $5, Premium $59 + $6

vendors.name: LBP (Licensed Building Practitioners)
vendors.tagline: NZ building-practitioner licensing — required to carry out or supervise Restricted Building Work
vendors.market_position: LBP (Licensed Building Practitioners) is New Zealand's licensing scheme for building practitioners, required to carry out or supervise Restricted Building Work (RBW) — the structural and weathertightness-critical work on homes. License classes cover carpentry, bricklaying and blocklaying, roofing, external plastering, foundations, site, and design, and the scheme is administered by MBIE.
vendors.nz_specific_note: LBP is the building-trades counterpart to EWRB (electrical) and PGDB (plumbing/gas/drainage) — together they form NZ's three core trade-licensing regimes. Builders doing Restricted Building Work must be licensed or supervised by an LBP, and the public LBP register is a consumer trust signal.
pros: Required for Restricted Building Work — clear regulatory pathway
Public LBP register provides a consumer trust signal
Multiple license classes cover the building trades
cons: Covers Restricted Building Work specifically (not all building work)
Multiple license classes can be confusing to navigate
vendors.typical_pricing: Annual licensing fees vary by license class

vendors.name: ServiceM8 (NZ)
vendors.tagline: Smart job management for small trade & service operators, with NZD pricing
vendors.market_position: The dominant job-management app for small trade/service operators (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, cleaning, pest control). It competes head-on with Tradify and Fergus at the solo-to-small-team end, differentiating on a generous free tier and flat per-plan (not per-user) pricing. It runs a dedicated NZ region with localised NZD pricing and is a long-standing Xero Award winner.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Dedicated servicem8.com/nz with NZD pricing and NZ support; native Xero + MYOB sync — the standard NZ accounting stack.
pros: Public NZD pricing with unlimited users on every paid plan
Genuinely usable free plan (1 user, 30 jobs/mo)
Built-in mobile payments, quoting, scheduling, SMS from job cards
cons: Pricing gated by jobs-per-month — high-volume teams jump tiers fast
Less suited to large project contractors
vendors.typical_pricing: Free $0 (30 jobs); Starter NZ$29, Growing NZ$79, Premium NZ$149, Premium Plus NZ$349/mo (unlimited users); 14-day trial

vendors.name: Simpro (NZ)
vendors.tagline: Heavyweight job management for mid-to-large project-based contractors
vendors.market_position: A heavyweight for mid-to-large project contractors (2–500+ staff). It sits above ServiceM8 and Tradify in depth — estimating, inventory, multi-stage projects, job costing and compliance — and has a real NZ footprint with an Auckland office and NZ support team.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Auckland office + NZ-based support; a Master Plumbers NZ partnership; integrates with Xero/MYOB. Best for established multi-staff firms, not solo tradies.
vendors.reputation_flag: Recurring user complaints about implementation complexity, setup cost, and lock-in contracts (Capterra NZ). No scandal — a fit/onboarding caveat for small operators.
pros: Deepest feature set for complex/project work
Physical Auckland office + NZ phone support
A Master Plumbers NZ member benefit
cons: Steep learning curve + high setup cost
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (custom by team size and add-ons)

vendors.name: AroFlo (NZ)
vendors.tagline: Full field-service management for established AU/NZ trade businesses
vendors.market_position: Full field-service management for established trade businesses across Australia and New Zealand. It sits between ServiceM8 (simple) and Simpro (enterprise) — strong on field-to-office workflow, compliance/safety forms, and asset and project management. It is now part of the global Simpro Group.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Explicit NZ support line and ANZ focus; Xero/MYOB integration standard. A fit for established field-service teams rather than solo operators.
pros: Strong field-service depth (compliance forms, assets, project Gantt, stock)
cons: AU-centric; lighter NZ localisation than ServiceM8
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based

vendors.name: Trade Me Services
vendors.tagline: NZ’s dominant marketplace — list a profile and homeowners contact you directly
vendors.market_position: New Zealand’s dominant online marketplace; its Services vertical lets tradies list a profile that homeowners browse and contact directly. Leads come from the huge incumbent audience rather than a Builderscrack-style bidding mechanic. Reach dwarfs niche tradie marketplaces, but it is a general directory, not a trades-specialist quote tool.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Iconic NZ-owned/operated, nationwide. Note the lead-gen relevance is Trade Me Services — Trade Me Jobs is a separate recruitment board for trade vacancies.
pros: Massive built-in NZ audience (largest of any option)
Self-serve listing, no per-lead bidding war
cons: General directory, not a trades-specialist quote engine
Listings expire (90 days); fees recur on relist
Onus on the provider to convert browsers
vendors.typical_pricing: Paid 90-day Services listing + optional upgrades; exact NZD not public (Trade Me dropped many success fees in 2026)

vendors.name: Localist
vendors.tagline: NZ-owned business directory with reviews — get found by locals
vendors.market_position: A large NZ-owned business directory (founded 2010, 200,000+ listings, 100,000+ reviews) where trades create a listing and get found by locals searching by category and region, backed by reviews. It is discovery-driven (search + reviews) rather than active job-bidding — Yellow-Pages-plus-reviews rather than a quote marketplace.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Explicitly NZ-owned (founded 2010) with dedicated city directories; runs a "free websites for Kiwi small businesses" initiative.
pros: Very large NZ footprint (200k+ listings, 100k+ reviews)
cons: Lower lead intent than "post your job"
vendors.typical_pricing: Free basic listing; paid/premium NZD tiers not public

vendors.name: FairTrades NZ
vendors.tagline: Kiwi-owned quote marketplace — post a job, verified local tradies quote
vendors.market_position: A Kiwi-owned quote marketplace where homeowners post a job free and verified local tradies get matched, quote, and manage the job via a dashboard — the closest model here to Builderscrack. It positions on fairness and transparency (side-by-side quote comparison). Scale appears small/early-stage.
vendors.nz_specific_note: "Kiwi owned and operated," verified Kiwi tradies; presence evidenced around Christchurch. Main caveat is unproven national scale, not reputation.
cons: Small/early-stage — limited evidence of nationwide scale
Tradie subscription NZD pricing not public
Brand awareness far below Builderscrack/Trade Me
vendors.typical_pricing: Free for homeowners; tradie subscription plans (NZD not public)

vendors.name: BuiltIn
vendors.tagline: NZ’s construction-insurance specialist broker — 20+ years
vendors.market_position: A specialist broker working exclusively with building and trade professionals, placing cover with multiple underwriters (Ando, IAG, Vero, QBE, Lloyds, DUAL, AIG). It offers the full trades stack: public liability, contract works, tools/equipment, commercial vehicles, professional indemnity (incl. LBP-specific), bonds and a Residential Completion Guarantee. The strongest trades-specific fit of the NZ insurers.
vendors.nz_specific_note: NZ-only construction-risk broker for 20+ years; explicit ties to NZ trades bodies, LBP-aware professional indemnity, and instant online quote/bind.
pros: Pure trades/construction specialist — deepest fit for builders/electricians/plumbers
Broad cover incl. contract works + Residential Completion Guarantee (rare)
Partners with NZ trades bodies (TANZ, NAWIC, etc.)
cons: Broker model = quote-based, no flat pricing
Smaller brand awareness vs mass-market insurers
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (instant online quotes via portal)

vendors.name: BizCover (NZ)
vendors.tagline: Online SME insurance comparison — instantly compare and buy tradie cover
vendors.market_position: An online small-business insurance comparison-and-buy platform (not a direct underwriter) working with a panel of NZ insurers, covering around 900 occupations including trades. Tradies instantly compare and buy public/product liability, professional indemnity and general liability online. It markets an explicit "Tradie Insurance NZ" product.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Dedicated .co.nz with an NZ insurer panel and 18,000+ NZ customers and an NZ-tailored "Tradie Insurance NZ" product — but an Australian-founded group.
pros: Transparent indicative pricing (public liability "~$30/mo on average")
Fully online instant quote/compare/buy, cancel anytime
cons: Comparison platform/broker, not a direct insurer
Self-serve = lighter advisory for complex risks
vendors.typical_pricing: From ~NZ$30/mo average for public liability ("guide only"); cover $1m–$20m

vendors.name: Thankyou Payroll
vendors.tagline: NZ cloud payroll + IRD PAYE intermediary — files PAYE/KiwiSaver for you
vendors.market_position: NZ-only cloud payroll for small businesses, charities and NFPs. It acts as an IRD-accredited PAYE Intermediary — filing and paying PAYE/KiwiSaver to IRD on the employer’s behalf, more hands-off than DIY tools. It competes with iPayroll/Smartly/PayHero on price and simplicity, with a social-enterprise/carbon-positive identity.
vendors.nz_specific_note: IRD payday filing explicitly automated; acts as an IRD PAYE Intermediary; handles KiwiSaver, PAYE and Holidays Act leave/holiday-pay calculations.
pros: Full PAYE-intermediary service — automated payday filing + IRD payments
Transparent capped NZD pricing (charged only for pays you run)
Free until first pay run; 100% free for registered charities
cons: Per-pay base fee can add up for weekly cycles
Transitioning to charity ownership (minor structural note)
vendors.typical_pricing: From ~NZ$8/mo; per-pay base + per-employee; monthly caps NZ$44–$399 by headcount; charities free

vendors.name: Crystal Payroll
vendors.tagline: NZ cloud payroll strong on irregular and shift hours
vendors.market_position: A long-running (10+ year) NZ-only cloud payroll, Auckland-based with NZ support. It is a strong fit for irregular hours and shift workers — relevant to trades with variable jobsite hours — and positions on low, stable pricing plus local service. It is also an IRD-accredited PAYE intermediary.
vendors.nz_specific_note: IRD payday filing automated with a filings dashboard; PAYE intermediary; KiwiSaver and holiday pay handled. "Proudly made in New Zealand," Auckland.
pros: Automated IRD payday filing with a dedicated dashboard
NZ-based support; low entry price held 10+ years
cons: Very small public review footprint (limited independent signal)
Plan tiers not clearly itemised publicly
vendors.typical_pricing: From NZ$19.90/mo (up to 5 employees), tiered by headcount; one-off NZ$50 registration

vendors.name: WorkSafe New Zealand
vendors.tagline: NZ’s primary work health & safety regulator — applies to every PCBU
vendors.market_position: The statutory health-and-safety regulator under the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA). It enforces duties on every PCBU — essentially every self-employed tradie and trades business — and is not optional, carrying inspection, improvement/prohibition-notice and prosecution powers. Construction is a WorkSafe priority sector.
vendors.nz_specific_note: Statutory basis is the HSWA 2015; mandatory for all PCBUs; obligations are legal duties, not membership. (The H&S regime is under government review/reform.)
pros: Compliance is the law (protects against prosecution/fines)
Free authoritative guidance + codes of practice
Demonstrable H&S systems win commercial contracts
cons: Broad "reasonably practicable" duties can be ambiguous
No marketable "badge" — it is baseline compliance
vendors.typical_pricing: Statutory regulator — no membership fee (government/levy funded)

vendors.name: Registered Master Builders Association
vendors.tagline: NZ’s most-recognised builder association — the Master Build Guarantee
vendors.market_position: A voluntary membership association for builders (established 1892), one of NZ’s two largest. Members can offer the Master Build Guarantee (10-Year / Multi-Unit) to homeowners — third-party cover for loss of deposit, non-completion, and materials/workmanship. Membership is opt-in (experience + business criteria) and is NOT a legal requirement (that is the mandatory LBP scheme).
vendors.nz_specific_note: Voluntary, not statutory — the consumer scheme is the Master Build Guarantee; distinct from the mandatory LBP scheme and from the separate NZCB (Halo guarantee). As of mid-2026 Master Builders and NZCB have NOT merged.
pros: Strong consumer trust/brand (House of the Year awards)
Useful where homeowners ask for the guarantee
cons: Annual fee + guarantee costs (passed to clients)
Voluntary — not a substitute for mandatory LBP
Member contract terms have drawn legal criticism
vendors.typical_pricing: Tiered membership fees (not fully public); Master Build Guarantee priced per project

vendors.name: Master Plumbers, Gasfitters & Drainlayers NZ
vendors.tagline: Peak body for plumbers, gasfitters & drainlayers — support + a trust mark
vendors.market_position: The largest national membership/peak body for plumbers, gasfitters and drainlayers (established 1901, 19 branches). It is voluntary — separate from mandatory PGDB licensing. Business membership requires being or employing a Certifying tradesperson running a business 6+ months, and offers business resources, a technical helpline, H&S tools and member discounts.
vendors.nz_specific_note: A voluntary peak body, NOT the regulator — the mandatory regulator is the PGDB (already listed). Master Plumbers complements it with business support and a consumer trust mark.
pros: Business support (HR, employment agreements, technical helpline, templates)
Member discounts (fuel, tools, software, Site Safe)
cons: Annual fee on top of mandatory PGDB costs
Voluntary — does not replace PGDB registration
Business tier gated to certifying-tradesperson owners
vendors.typical_pricing: Apprentice NZ$40 +GST/yr; Associate Individual NZ$80 +GST/yr; Business membership quote (not public)
